request.product_brief: Launch Desk is an internal planning assistant for engineering teams. We are launching a first version that turns rough ideas into actionable release plans, risk registers, owner checklists, and launch copy.
request.audience: Engineering leads, PMs, release managers, and support
constraints: Support must have FAQ and escalation notes before launch

report.title: Launch Desk v1 Internal Launch Plan
report.summary: Launch Desk is ready to proceed toward the 2026-06-25 internal launch, with strong brief clarity, defined audience, named constraints, and enough assets to support launch communications. The main watchout is timing: the launch is close, so final security review, support readiness, rollback criteria, and go/no-go ownership should be locked immediately.
assumptions: The product brief is detailed enough to infer launch scope.
Primary audience is Engineering leads, PMs, release managers, and support.
Launch date is 2026-06-25.
This is an internal launch.
No blocking gaps were identified by the planning tools.

prioritized_plan.action: Lock the launch scope, success criteria, and go/no-go criteria.
prioritized_plan.reason: The team needs one source of truth before execution and launch decision-making.

prioritized_plan.action: Confirm the exact launch window and decision owner.
prioritized_plan.reason: A near-term launch needs a named decision owner and clear decision point.

prioritized_plan.action: Confirm final integration, QA, deployment, monitoring, release branch, and rollback dependencies.
prioritized_plan.reason: Late integration, release branch drift, or unclear rollback ownership can become launch blockers.

prioritized_plan.action: Complete required security review and document any launch conditions.
prioritized_plan.reason: Security review is an explicit launch constraint and must be cleared before release.

prioritized_plan.action: Finalize FAQ, escalation notes, known-issue responses, and support handoff.
prioritized_plan.reason: Support must have FAQ and escalation notes before launch.

prioritized_plan.action: Run final smoke tests, log open defects, and verify rollback trigger criteria.
prioritized_plan.reason: A launch without explicit test results and rollback criteria is a release risk.

prioritized_plan.action: Validate screenshots, launch visuals, and any in-product surfaces against final product scope.
prioritized_plan.reason: Launch assets need to match what users will see in v1.

prioritized_plan.action: Finalize Slack, email, and release notes copy and align send timing.
prioritized_plan.reason: Copy should be consistent across launch channels and tailored to the internal audience.

prioritized_plan.action: Work through each constraint and assign a direct owner for completion.
prioritized_plan.reason: Constraints can become the hidden critical path if not explicitly owned.

risk_register.risk: Security review is required but not explicitly confirmed complete.
risk_register.mitigation: Assign Security as owner, complete review before go/no-go, and document any conditions.

risk_register.risk: Launch timing is close, leaving limited buffer for late defects or asset changes.
risk_register.mitigation: Hold a final readiness checkpoint, freeze scope, and escalate blockers immediately.

risk_register.risk: Production downtime is not allowed.
risk_register.mitigation: Validate deployment steps, monitoring, and rollback plan before launch.

risk_register.risk: Support may not be fully prepared if FAQ and escalation notes remain in draft state.
risk_register.mitigation: Finalize FAQ, publish escalation path, and share known-issue responses before launch.

risk_register.risk: Rollback criteria may be visible but not yet explicit enough for launch-day execution.
risk_register.mitigation: Name rollback owner, define rollback trigger, and verify rollback steps during readiness review.

checklist: Confirm launch scope and decision owner.
Lock the go/no-go criteria.
Verify deployment steps and monitoring.
Confirm rollback and rollback owner.
Execute the final smoke test plan.
Log any open defects with a release decision.
Finalize the main launch message.
Adapt copy for Slack, email, and release notes.
Prepare the support handoff note.
Share the FAQ, escalation notes, and issue response plan.
Validate screenshots, visuals, and launch artifacts.
Confirm the assets match the latest product scope.
Review each constraint and assign a direct owner.
Confirm any missing approvals or dependencies.
Schedule the final readiness checkpoint.
Make the rollback trigger explicit.

launch_copy_suggestions.audience: Engineering leads, PMs, release managers, and support

Launch Desk turns rough launch ideas into actionable release plans, risk registers, owner checklists, and launch copy. Key watchouts: no production downtime, required security review, and support readiness. Please review your owner checklist and reply with any blockers before the readiness checkpoint.

launch_copy_suggestions.audience: Engineering leads, PMs, release managers, and support
launch_copy_suggestions.draft:
Subject: Launch Desk v1 internal launch plan

Team, we are preparing to launch Launch Desk v1 internally on 2026-06-25. This first version helps engineering teams turn rough ideas into release plans, risk registers, owner checklists, and launch copy.

Before launch, we need to confirm security review completion, no-downtime deployment readiness, final FAQ and escalation notes, and rollback criteria.

Please review your assigned checklist and raise any blockers before the final readiness review.

launch_copy_suggestions.audience: Engineering leads, PMs, release managers, and support
launch_copy_suggestions.draft:
Launch Desk v1 is an internal planning assistant for engineering teams. It helps convert rough launch ideas into actionable release plans, risk registers, owner checklists, and channel-specific launch copy.

This release is intended for engineering leads, PMs, release managers, and support. Launch guardrails include no production downtime, completed security review, and support readiness with FAQ and escalation notes.

top_gaps: Launch timing is close; final readiness and launch-window details should be confirmed immediately.

rubric.notes: The brief is specific enough to guide execution.
Audience is concrete.
Launch is close, so final checkpoint discipline matters.
Product screenshots, FAQ draft, release notes outline, and internal rollout checklist are available.
Constraints are named: no production downtime, security review required, and support readiness required.
A release safety path is visible, but rollback trigger should be made explicit.
Copy can be tailored for Slack, email, and release notes.

report.next_action: Schedule the final readiness checkpoint and confirm three items first: security review owner/status, exact launch window plus decision owner, and rollback trigger plus rollback owner.

request.product_brief: Launch Desk is a planning assistant for engineering teams. Turn a rough launch idea into an actionable release plan with priorities, risks, owners, and channel copy.

report.title: Launch Desk Release Plan
report.summary: Launch Desk is on track for a 2026-06-25 launch to engineering leadership and release owners. Readiness score is 90/100 with a go verdict. The main watchout is timing: the launch is close, so final scope, decision ownership, security review, rollback criteria, and no-downtime deployment checks should be locked immediately.
assumptions: The product brief is detailed enough to infer scope.
Primary audience is engineering leadership and release owners.
Launch date is 2026-06-25.
Product screenshots and FAQ draft are available for launch preparation.

prioritized_plan.action: Lock the launch scope and success criteria.
prioritized_plan.reason: The team needs one source of truth before scheduling execution work.

prioritized_plan.action: Confirm the exact launch window and decision owner.
prioritized_plan.reason: Near-term launches need a decision point and a named approver.

prioritized_plan.action: Confirm the last integration, QA, and release branch dependencies.
prioritized_plan.reason: Release blockers usually surface in late integration or environment drift.

prioritized_plan.reason: Audience clarity and asset quality determine whether the launch feels ready.

prioritized_plan.action: Prepare FAQ, escalation paths, and known-issue responses.
prioritized_plan.reason: Support readiness is the fastest way to reduce launch friction.

prioritized_plan.action: Run the readiness rubric and verify rollback criteria.
prioritized_plan.reason: A launch without a rollback path is still a risk, not a plan.

risk_register.risk: No production downtime constraint may be missed if deployment, monitoring, or rollback steps are not explicit.
risk_register.mitigation: Engineering should verify deployment plan, monitoring, rollback trigger, and rollback owner before launch.

risk_register.mitigation: Hold an immediate readiness checkpoint and freeze scope unless a go/no-go owner approves changes.

request.product_brief: Launch Desk exports the visible launch plan directly from the current result panel so release owners can download markdown or json without reopening history.
constraints: Export must work immediately after final response

report.title: Launch plan: Current result panel exports
report.summary: Launch Desk will let engineering release owners export the visible launch plan directly from the current result panel as Markdown or JSON, without reopening saved history. The plan is currently a go based on the provided brief, constraints, assets, and launch date.
assumptions: The product brief is detailed enough to infer launch scope.
Primary audience is engineering release owners.
Launch date is 2026-06-26.
Export formats are Markdown and JSON.
Existing saved history export behavior must remain unchanged.

prioritized_plan.action: Lock launch scope and success criteria for exporting the visible result panel.
prioritized_plan.reason: The team needs one source of truth before final execution.

prioritized_plan.action: Confirm implementation, integration points, release branch status, and that export works immediately after final response generation.
prioritized_plan.reason: The core launch promise depends on the export being available without reopening history.

prioritized_plan.action: Run smoke and regression tests for current result panel export and saved history export.
prioritized_plan.reason: Both the new export path and the existing history export path must work independently.

prioritized_plan.action: Work through each stated constraint and assign direct verification owners.
prioritized_plan.reason: The two constraints are the critical release guardrails.

prioritized_plan.action: Verify in-product surface, export affordance, labels, and any screenshots used in release communications.
prioritized_plan.reason: Release owners need to understand where to export from and which format to choose.

prioritized_plan.action: Prepare FAQ, escalation path, and known-issue responses.
prioritized_plan.reason: Support readiness reduces launch friction if export behavior is misunderstood or fails.

prioritized_plan.action: Finalize Slack and release note copy.
prioritized_plan.reason: Comms should clearly explain the workflow improvement for engineering release owners.

prioritized_plan.action: Run final readiness check and verify rollback criteria.
prioritized_plan.reason: A clear rollback path is required before launch.

risk_register.risk: Export is not available immediately after the final response is rendered.
risk_register.mitigation: Add explicit QA coverage for exporting from the current result panel immediately after final response completion.

risk_register.risk: Saved history export regresses while adding current panel export.
risk_register.mitigation: Run regression tests against existing history storage and saved history export flows before go/no-go.

risk_register.risk: Users confuse current result panel export with saved history export.
risk_register.mitigation: Use clear in-product labels and release copy that distinguishes current result export from saved history export.

risk_register.risk: Markdown or JSON output does not match expected visible plan content.
risk_register.mitigation: Validate exported files against the visible panel content during smoke testing.

checklist: Confirm launch scope and decision owner.
Lock go/no-go criteria.
Verify deployment steps and monitoring.
Confirm rollback and rollback owner.
Validate export availability immediately after final response.
Execute final smoke test plan.
Log any open defects with a release decision.
Regression test saved history export.
Finalize main launch message.
Adapt copy for Slack and release notes.
Prepare support handoff note.
Share FAQ or issue response plan.
Validate screenshots, visuals, and launch artifacts.
Confirm assets match latest product scope.
Check export affordance and labels in the current result panel.
Review each constraint and assign a direct owner.
Confirm any missing approvals or dependencies.

launch_copy_suggestions.draft: Launch Desk update: release owners can now export the visible launch plan directly from the current result panel as Markdown or JSON, without reopening history. Target launch: 2026-06-26. Key guardrails: export must work immediately after the final response, and saved history export must remain intact. Please review your owner checklist and reply with blockers.

Launch Desk now supports exporting the visible launch plan directly from the current result panel. Engineering release owners can download Markdown or JSON immediately after the final response, without reopening saved history. Existing saved history export remains intact.

rubric.notes: The brief is specific enough to guide execution.
Audience is concrete.
Launch date is set for 2026-06-26.
Existing history storage and current result panel are listed.
Constraints are named and should be verified directly.
Release safety path is visible, with QA and rollback verification assigned.
Slack and release note copy are drafted.

report.next_action: PM should confirm final go/no-go criteria and assign Engineering + QA owners for immediate-export verification and saved-history export regression.

prioritized_plan.action: Validate any screenshots, visuals, or launch artifacts against the latest product scope.
prioritized_plan.reason: Asset quality helps prevent confusion at launch.

risk_register.risk: Security review is not completed before the target launch date.
risk_register.mitigation: Assign a named security review owner, due date, and escalation path immediately. Do not launch until review is complete.

risk_register.risk: Customer-facing downtime occurs during or after deployment.
risk_register.mitigation: Engineering must confirm deployment steps, monitoring, rollback owner, and rollback triggers before launch.

risk_register.risk: Late integration, QA, or release branch issue blocks launch readiness.
risk_register.mitigation: Confirm final integration status and run the smoke test plan before the readiness review.

risk_register.risk: Open defects are not clearly triaged before go/no-go.
risk_register.mitigation: QA should log each open defect with severity, release decision, and owner.

risk_register.risk: Engineering leads and release owners do not know what changed or who owns follow-up.
risk_register.mitigation: Publish concise Slack, email, and release note updates with owners, constraints, and escalation path.

risk_register.risk: FAQ and known-issue responses are incomplete at launch.
risk_register.mitigation: Support should complete the internal FAQ outline and include escalation guidance before launch comms.

checklist: Confirm launch scope and decision owner.
Lock the go/no-go criteria.
Verify deployment steps and monitoring.
Confirm rollback and rollback owner.
Execute the final smoke test plan.
Log any open defects with a release decision.
Finalize the main launch message.
Adapt copy for each required channel.
Prepare the support handoff note.
Share the customer-facing FAQ or issue response plan.
Validate screenshots, visuals, and launch artifacts.
Confirm the assets match the latest product scope.
Review each constraint and assign a direct owner.
Confirm any missing approvals or dependencies.

launch_copy_suggestions.draft:
Launch Desk update: we are targeting 2026-07-02 for the internal Smoke test 1781779073 release. Goal: help engineering teams plan small internal releases with owners, risks, readiness checks, and launch copy.

Assets in progress: draft release notes and internal FAQ outline.
Constraint watchouts: security review before launch and no customer-facing downtime.

Please review your owner checklist and reply with blockers, especially security review, rollback, QA, or deployment risks.

Subject: Launch plan: Smoke test 1781779073

Team,

We are preparing the internal Launch Desk release for 2026-07-02. This release should help engineering teams plan small internal launches with clear owners, risks, readiness checks, and launch copy.

Current assets:
- Draft release notes
- Internal FAQ outline

Key constraints:
- Security review must be complete before launch
- No customer-facing downtime

Please review the plan, confirm your owner checklist, and reply with any blockers before the readiness review.

Smoke test 1781779073

Launch Desk now supports planning for small internal engineering releases, including prioritized actions, owner checklists, risk tracking, readiness summaries, follow-up questions, and channel-ready launch copy.

Target launch: 2026-07-02
Audience: engineering leads and release owners
Assets: draft release notes and internal FAQ outline
Guardrails: security review before launch and no customer-facing downtime

rubric.notes: The brief is specific enough to guide execution.
Audience is concrete.
Launch window is 14 day(s) away.
Enough launch assets are listed.
Constraints are named.
A release safety path is visible.
Copy can be tailored.

report.next_action: Assign named owners and due dates for security review, go/no-go decision, and rollback before continuing launch execution.
